notes.note: Venusia (modern Venosa) was a town in Apulia on the Via Appia, the birthplace of the poet Horace; Cicero writes while passing through its territory on his way home in October 47 BC. This brisk note ordering the Tusculan house made ready marks his return to ordinary life after Caesar's pardon.

notes.anchor: If there is no tub in the bath
notes.note: The labrum was a large basin holding water for the hot room of a Roman bath suite; Cicero's instruction shows the everyday household detail of preparing the villa for guests.
